checks.finding: Actors (human owner, orchestrating agent, reviewer subagents, cross-model panelists), states (Domain-Model-Status: Pending|Reviewed|Approved), and inputs (text/Markdown path/issue URL seeds, investigation.md for reverse mode) are defined concretely enough in requirements.md Roles/Permissions and AC-004/AC-007 that two implementers would build the same observable behavior.

AC-008 (inject Bounded-Context/aggregate refs when Approved model exists) and AC-010 (byte-identical output, all hooks/gates skipped when domain/ absent) are consistent because they are gated on mutually exclusive preconditions (domain/ present+Approved vs. domain/ absent); the Edge Cases 'not Approved: warn and proceed without injection' entry is consistent with, not contradictory to, AC-008. No direct conflict found across goals, non-goals, ACs, or constraints in requirements.md.

requirements.md Edge Cases (line 147-149) states: 'Cross-model panelist unavailable (offline/local env): record panelist-unavailable, set requires_human_decision (fail toward human, never silently pass).' This is a distinct failure mode from the cross-model mismatch path covered by AC-006/TEST-006 in acceptance-tests.md. Searching acceptance-tests.md for 'panelist' yields no matches — there is no AC or TEST-ID for the panelist-unavailable path. A test author cannot derive an observable acceptance criterion or fixture for this failure mode from acceptance-tests.md alone, and this belongs to spec review because acceptance-tests.md is a Phase 1 artifact under this gate's scope, not a later quality-gate concern. Severity is Major because it is a missing observable acceptance criterion for a named failure mode, not a contradiction that makes the spec unreviewable.

Open Questions OQ-R1 and OQ-R2 are explicitly marked non-blocking with an owner (human) and a stated v1 interim default, satisfying the resolvable-or-flagged pattern. The Assumptions section (v1.8.0 structure reuse, cross-model panelist reuse, version-locked plugin bump) contains narrow technical/infrastructure assumptions rather than unresolved product-behavior questions, so they do not block design or task decomposition.

AC-007 and AC-014, backed by the Roles/Permissions table and Security Boundaries table, define a testable human-approval boundary: only a human sets Domain-Model-Status: Approved, a hook guard performs line-diff rejection of agent-set Approved lines (acceptance-tests.md UI Integration Checklist AC-007 bullet), and any post-approval edit under domain/ resets status to Pending pending re-review (AC-014, TEST-014). This boundary is concrete and testable, not merely asserted.

Because the panelist-unavailable failure mode (requirements.md lines 147-149) has no corresponding AC or TEST-ID, an implementation-policy/design reviewer inheriting this spec would have to invent the acceptance surface (what file records 'panelist-unavailable', what fixture proves requires_human_decision is set on this path) rather than trace it to a written acceptance criterion, which is exactly the invented-product-behavior risk this check screens for. This is the same root cause as the EDGE-CASE-COVERAGE finding but represents a distinct downstream failure mode (design/task readiness vs. test-fixture derivability), so it is reported separately per the check definitions.
